guidelinesText:
UNDERWRITING GUIDELINES – SMALL COMMERCIAL PACKAGE

TARGET CLASSES
- Casual dining restaurants
- Coffee shops and cafes
- Small bakeries
- Boutique retail stores
- Florists
- Small professional offices

BUSINESS REQUIREMENTS
- Minimum years in business: 3
- Minimum years with current owner: 2
- Maximum annual revenue: $5,000,000
- Maximum employees: 40

PROPERTY REQUIREMENTS
- Building age must be 40 years or newer OR have updated electrical and plumbing
- Roof must be less than 20 years old
- Fire extinguishers required and serviced
- Smoke detectors required
- Sprinkler system preferred but not required
- No structural damage

OCCUPANCY
- Owner occupied preferred (50% or greater)
- Multi-tenant buildings acceptable if tenants are low hazard
- No manufacturing or heavy industrial operations allowed

RESTAURANT REQUIREMENTS
Acceptable:
- Casual dining
- Coffee shops
- Bakeries

Conditions:
- Deep fryers allowed only with automatic suppression systems
- Commercial hood suppression system required
- Liquor sales allowed only if less than 40% of revenue

DECLINE IF
- Nightclubs
- Bars where liquor is more than 50% of revenue
- Hookah lounges

PROPERTY VALUE LIMITS
- Maximum building value: $3,000,000
- Maximum contents value: $1,000,000
- Business interruption coverage allowed

LOSS HISTORY
Acceptable:
- Maximum 2 losses within the past 5 years
- No single loss greater than $100,000

DECLINE IF
- Fire loss within the past 3 years
- Pattern of slip and fall claims

INELIGIBLE RISKS
- Vacant properties
- Cannabis operations
- Firearms sales
- Hazardous chemical storage
- Nightclubs
- Adult entertainment businesses

guidelinesText:
Sample Property Underwriting Guidelines
Pelorus Demo Carrier – Property Program
The following underwriting guidelines outline the eligibility criteria for commercial property submissions.
These rules are used to triage incoming submissions and determine whether a risk is acceptable, refer,
or decline.
1. Roof age must be less than 20 years.
2. Total insured value must not exceed $1,000,000.
3. Property must be owner-occupied.
4. Maximum two claims in the past five years.
5. Buildings constructed before 1900 are ineligible.
6. Vacant properties for more than 60 days are ineligible.
7. Sprinkler systems required for buildings larger than 25,000 square feet.
8. Properties located in FEMA Flood Zone A or V require underwriting referral.
9. Restaurants with deep fryer operations require automatic fire suppression systems.
10. Properties within 1 mile of the coastline require underwriting referral.
Example Fact Acceptable Range
Roof Age (Years) < 20
Total Insured Value ≤ $1,000,000
Claims (Last 5 Years) ≤ 2
Owner Occupancy Required

## MERIDIAN SPECIALTY BROKERS, INC. Commercial Insurance Brokerage | TO: | Commercial Property Underwriting — New England Program | |-|-| | FROM: | James R. Callahan, CPCU · Senior Vice President | | DATE: | March 14, 2025 | | RE: | NEW BUSINESS SUBMISSION — Harborview Industrial Partners LLC | | LINES: | Commercial Property · General Liability · Umbrella | | EFF. DATE: | May 1, 2025 | Dear Underwriting Team, I am pleased to present Harborview Industrial Partners LLC for your consideration on a new commercial property, general liability, and commercial umbrella program effective May 1, 2025. Harborview is an established industrial real estate owner-operator headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island, with properties concentrated in the greater southern New England market. The account represents four (4) operating locations comprising approximately 187,000 square feet of light industrial, flex, and warehouse space. The portfolio has been with its current carrier, Granite State Mutual, for the past seven years. We are remarketing this account as Granite State has announced their withdrawal from New England commercial property effective June 1, 2025 — this is a market exit, not an underwriting non-renewal, and we can provide written confirmation of that if required. I want to be upfront about a few items so there are no surprises in your review: •Location 3 (Providence Cold Storage Annex) has a roof that was replaced in 2006 — making it approximately 19 years old. We are aware this is approaching the 20-year threshold carried by many carriers. The insured has obtained a current roof inspection (included in this submission) and reports the membrane is in good condition with no active ponding or seam issues. •There was one fire loss in 2021 at Location 2 (the Pawtucket Flex Center) totaling $247,500 — the result of an electrical fault in a tenant's space. The tenant has since vacated and the space has been re-tenanted with a lower-hazard occupancy. The panel involved was replaced as part of the restoration. Full loss details are in the attached loss runs. •Location 4 (the Bristol Street Warehouse) has a small section of the building — approximately 800 sq ft of the 42,000 sq ft total — that includes original 1962 construction used as a maintenance shop. This section is separately fused and the insured is aware it predates modern wiring standards. An electrician inspection was completed in February 2025. The overall account has a strong 7-year loss history with only the one fire loss noted above. The properties are well-maintained, professionally managed, and the portfolio has zero open claims as of today. The insured carries an active sprinkler system at three of four locations and maintains central station monitoring throughout the CONFIDENTIAL — For Underwriting Purposes Only · Harborview Industrial Partners LLC Page 1 portfolio. I have attached the following documents for your review: 1. This cover letter 2. Schedule of Values (SOV) — all four locations 3. ACORD 125 Commercial Insurance Application 4. Five-year certified loss runs (Granite State Mutual, carrier-certified) 5. Broker Terms & Conditions Request 6. Roof inspection report — Location 3 (dated February 2025) 7. Electrician inspection report — Location 4 maintenance shop (dated February 2025) We are requesting a response by April 4, 2025 to allow adequate time for binding prior to the May 1 effective date. I am happy to schedule a call to discuss any aspect of this account. Thank you for your time and consideration. James R. Callahan, CPCU Senior Vice President — Commercial Lines Meridian Specialty Brokers, Inc. Direct: (401) 555-0182 · Mobile: (401) 555-0247 jcallahan@meridianbrokers.com One Financial Plaza, Suite 1400 · Providence, RI 02903 This transmission is intended only for the named recipient and may contain confidential and proprietary information. Both inspectors are licensed in Rhode Island and Massachusetts respectively. | REPORT A — ROOF INSPECTION · LOCATION 3 · 90 Allens Ave, Providence RI | REPORT A — ROOF INSPECTION · LOCATION 3 · 90 Allens Ave, Providence RI | |-|-| | Inspector: | Harrington & Sons Roofing Consultants LLC | | Inspector License: | RI Roofing Contractor License #RC-00441 | | Date of Inspection: | February 7, 2025 | | Property: | 90 Allens Avenue, Providence, RI 02905 | | Roof Type: | EPDM Membrane — Single-Ply, Fully Adhered | | Roof Area (approx): | 42,000 sq ft — one continuous flat roof | | Year Installed: | 2006 (approximately 19 years old at time of inspection) | | Drainage: | Interior drains (4) + perimeter scuppers (8) | ## FINDINGS: •Membrane Condition — GOOD: The EPDM membrane is intact with no observed punctures, blisters, or active splits. Seams were probed at 12 locations and found adhered with no lifting or separation detected. •Drainage — ADEQUATE: All four interior drains are clear and functional. Scuppers are open and show no signs of obstruction. No evidence of active ponding water or birdbath formations on the membrane surface. • Flashings — GOOD: Perimeter flashings and pitch pockets are intact. No open seams at parapet walls. Counter-flashings seated. •Penetrations — GOOD: All roof penetrations (HVAC curbs, conduit, drains) are properly flashed and sealed. No open penetrations identified. • HVAC Curbs — FAIR: Three of the five HVAC curbs show minor sealant cracking at the curb-to-membrane interface. Recommend re-sealing with approved EPDM lap sealant within 12 months. Not currently a leak source but proactive maintenance advised. INSPECTOR CONCLUSION: This roof, while approaching the end of its expected 20–25 year service life, is in good condition for its age. With proactive HVAC curb re-sealing and continued annual maintenance, this roof can reasonably be expected to perform adequately for an additional 3–5 years without replacement. Immediate replacement is NOT recommended at this time. Inspector estimates replacement would be appropriate approximately 2027–2028 based on current condition. February 7, 2025 Robert Harrington, Principal Inspector CONFIDENTIAL — For Underwriting Purposes Only · Harborview Industrial Partners LLC Page 1 ## Harrington & Sons Roofing Consultants LLC CONFIDENTIAL — For Underwriting Purposes Only · Harborview Industrial Partners LLC Page 2 ## REPORT B — ELECTRICAL INSPECTION · LOCATION 4 · 1100 Bristol St, New Bedford MA | Inspector: | Southeastern MA Electrical Inspections LLC | |-|-| | Inspector License: | MA Licensed Electrician — License #E-18844; MA Electrical Inspector | | Date of Inspection: | February 18, 2025 | | Property: | 1100 Bristol Street, New Bedford, MA 02745 | | Purpose: | Inspection of pre-1994 maintenance shop wiring — approximately 800 sq ft | | Scope: | Visual inspection + load testing of maintenance shop electrical sub-panel and
branch circuits | ## BACKGROUND: The main building (41,200 sq ft) was substantially renovated in 1994, including full electrical replacement with copper Romex wiring and a new Square D main panel (400A service). The maintenance shop (approximately 800 sq ft, attached to the main structure at the northeast corner) was not part of the 1994 renovation and retains its original 1962-era wiring fed by a separate fused sub-panel. ## FINDINGS — MAINTENANCE SHOP: • Wiring Type — ORIGINAL 1962: The maintenance shop contains what appears to be original cloth-insulated wiring on 12 circuits. Wiring is 12-gauge copper with cloth-and-rubber insulation typical of 1955–1965 construction. • Sub-Panel — FAIR: The maintenance shop is fed by a 60-amp fused sub-panel (not a breaker panel). The fuses are properly rated for the circuits. The panel itself is serviceable but dated. • Insulation Condition — FAIR/CONCERN: The cloth insulation on older circuits is brittle in several locations, consistent with age. Two circuits show insulation cracking near junction boxes. These circuits should be treated as a priority for replacement. • Load Utilization — LOW: The maintenance shop is used for light tool storage and occasional maintenance work (hand tools, lighting). Load utilization is approximately 15% of panel capacity. No heavy equipment or continuous-load machinery is on these circuits. •Ground Fault / Arc Fault Protection — ABSENT: No GFCI or AFCI protection on the aged circuits, consistent with pre-NEC 2000 construction. INSPECTOR CONCLUSION: The maintenance shop wiring presents an elevated condition relative to modern standards due to its age and cloth insulation. The two circuits with cracked insulation near junction boxes should be replaced promptly. Given the low load utilization and nature of use (light storage, infrequent light maintenance), the inspector does not characterize this as an imminent fire hazard but does recommend rewiring the maintenance shop within 12 months as a risk management priority. RECOMMENDATION: Rewire the 800 sq ft maintenance shop — estimated cost $8,000–$12,000. In the interim, the two identified deteriorated circuits should be de-energized.
